info.title: Batch API
info.description:
The Batch Autonomous Service (BatchAS) allows CRUD operations on Batch items.
- A batch has:
    - a set of properties like name and description that can be updated
    - a definition which consist of a generic node hierarchy; each node in a hierarchy has an associated collection of blobs; typically it is used as generic representation of the analytical data
    - a set of relations with other data records
    - a type which is set at create time and can not be changed later post batch creation
    - a folder where it is placed into
    - a set of read only properties which are reflecting various lifecycle aspects like creation date time, modification date time, state, etc.
- waters_connect applications use different terminology for batch entities most used one being **analysis**

get.summary: Gets a list of last modified batches by the user.
get.description: * For each batch, the properties of the last version (Draft or Published) are returned. The list is ordered descending by modified date time. * A draft batch is a preliminary version of a batch, not considered in final state and not considered a compliance record. * A published batch is a batch in final state and it is considered a compliance record. * An empty collection will be returned when the user does not have access to the folder or the user does not have permission to view offline folders and the folder is offline. * The limit value must be greater than 0. * Batches that are being archived will not be returned.

parameters.name: dataRecordType
parameters.description: The batch data record type

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of records to return

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description:
The sub-resources to expand.

Valid options are: definition - includes definition in the response.

Note: This should be used with caution as it may cause slow response.

get.summary: Gets the batches of a given type from a given folder. Can be filtered as well by user and state.
get.description: * The latest version of the batches are returned if no 'state' filter is provided. Otherwise, batch versions with the given state are returned. * Expand parameter controls the retrieval of the definition. * A draft batch is a preliminary version of a batch, not considered in final state and not considered a compliance record. * A published batch is a batch in final state and it is considered a compliance record. * The folder must be readable for the user. * Batches that are being archived will not be returned.

parameters.name: allUsers
parameters.description: When set to false, returns versions of records where the creator/modifier of the version is the current user. Default is true.

post.summary: Restores the given Superseded version as current version.
post.description: The given version is recreated as last version in Draft or Published state, as controlled by keepInDraft property. * The folder must be writable for the user. * A writable folder is a folder in online, offline or closed to capture status. * The user has assigned access to the batch folder. * Write access involves view permission. * If the new version remains in Draft state, an audit log entry is created, including the version number of the given version in the details. * If the new version is published, an audit log entry is created and the reason is checked against the Modify audit trail reason policy. * The allowed actions of an user in a given folder and the potential audit policy information can be determined using '/folder/{folderId}/actions'. * If the source version is the published one, use the draft endpoint (POST /batch-versions/{id}/draft). * A batch cannot be restored while it is being archived. * The restored batch is marked as ineligible for archival (status: NeverArchive) only if the source batch is also ineligible. Otherwise, the restored batch's archival status remains unset (status: NoAutoExport).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The batch version identifier.

requestBody.description: Details of the batch version to be restored.

201.description: Batch version successfully restored. Properties of the new current batch version are returned in the response body.

patch.summary: Allows partial update of the batch.
patch.description:
* A batch cannot be related to an item that is being archived.
* Allows relations with any UDF item types, except for Component Calibration and Diagnostic Data.
* Allows marking a batch as not eligible for archival by setting the ArchiveEligible flag to false. This change is irreversible once applied.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description:
The entity is only altered if it has a matching ETag. Otherwise a 412 response is returned.          
             When If-Match is missing 428 Precondition Required response is returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The batch version identifier.

requestBody.description:
The patch request document.

Contains PatchRequest object as described by https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6902.

The path member of the PatchDocument models can be set with values like: /name, /description, /archiveEligible, /definition/name, /definition/genericNodes, /definition/blobStorage, /relations/id, /relations/relatedRecordId

The operations are executed sequentially. For example, for repeated remove, the relation index will decrease by one.

delete.summary: Discard a draft version of the batch.
delete.description:
Warning Consumers should not call this endpoint for draft versions of batches/analyses containing impactful changes when the global policy setting prevents permanent discard of such changes. The global policy setting can be determined via /analyses-draft-discard-policy.
  • The folder must be writable for the user.
  • An audit log will be created which may need to include a reason collected from the user, according with 'Discard edits' action of the 'Data folder reason policy' associated with the folder the batch is stored into.
  • The allowed actions of an user in a given folder and the potential audit policy information can be determined using '/folder/{folderId}/actions'.

post.summary: Create a new version in draft of an existing published batch
post.description: * The folder must be writable for the user. * A writable folder is a folder in online, offline or closed to capture status. * The user has assigned access to the batch folder. * Write access involves view permission. * A draft batch is a preliminary version of a batch, not considered in final state and not considered a compliance record. * A published batch is a batch in final state and it is considered a compliance record. * An audit log might be created. * A new version in draft cannot be created while the existing batch is being archived. * Allows relations with any UDF item types, except for Component Calibration and Diagnostic Data. * Allows marking a batch as not eligible for archival by setting the ArchiveEligible flag to false (if a valid payload is sent). This change is irreversible once applied.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The batch version identifier.

requestBody.description:
Optional payload, if a payload is used then a new draft version is created and updated with the payload and relations with other types of data.
In case the payload is not passed in, then the previous batch information and its relations are preserved.
The payload is represented by the batch itself and has a generic representation data model, defined by the user.

201.description: Success. Returns the properties of the new draft batch version.

post.summary: Creates a copy of the batch.
post.description:
The given version is copied as to a new record in a Published state.
* The destination folder must be writable for the user.
* The new version remains in Published state, an audit log entry is created, including the version number of the given version in the details.
